name: Algarvio Portuguese
notes: Algarvio Portuguese, southernmost continental dialect (Faro, PortimГЈo, Lagos, Sagres). Modelled features: (1) DEFINING Barlavento feature вҖ” fronting of the stressed labio-velar /u/ вҶ’ [y] (tudoвҶ’[ЛҲtydu], lumeвҶ’[ЛҲlymЙЁ], mudoвҶ’[ЛҲmydu]); encoded as the allophone rule ALG_U_FRONTING, stressed nucleus only (Cintra 1971; Brissos 2014; Segura da Cruz 1989). (2) /ej/ вҶ’ [e] and /ow/ вҶ’ [o] monophthongs (leiteвҶ’[ЛҲletЙЁ], outroвҶ’[ЛҲotЙҫu]). (3) Southern sibilant voicing вҖ” word-final /s/ вҶ’ [К’] (masвҶ’[maК’], muitos amigosвҶ’[ЛҲmЕ©jМғtuК’ ЙҗЛҲmiЙЎuК’]); encoded in positional_graphemes s/codaвҶ’[К’], word_finalвҶ’[К’]. This is the STRONGEST realisation of the /К’/-for-/z/ external sandhi (word-final s before a vowel-initial word вҶ’ [К’]; the 'Tajaver' pronunciation of estГЎs a ver вҶ’ [eЛҲКғtaК’ ЛҲЙҗ ЛҲvЙӣЙҫ]; Portuguese With Leo, 'The 8 accents', native-speaker source), shared more weakly by SГЈo Miguel (see pt-PT-x-acores, which applies it prevocalically only) and variable in Coimbra, while Lisbon and the North keep [z]. Because the Algarve realisation is categorical across all word-final positions, it is modelled by the positional word_final [К’] map (not a separate prevocalic sandhi rule) and therefore already surfaces [К’] before a following vowel. (4) Open stressed vowel qualities [Йӣ, Й”]. (5) Dark coda /l/вҶ’[ЕӮ] and coda /s,z/вҶ’[Кғ,К’] inherited from the pt-PT base allophone_rules. NOT MODELLED: the older/rural apico-alveolar sibilant [sМҘ] (articulatory, not phoneme-level simulable вҖ” Cintra 1971 flags this class of feature as non-modellable); the broader stressed [a]вҶ’[Йӣ] / [Й”]вҶ’[oМқ] chain shift (context-conditioned; see ALG_U_FRONTING notes). No betacism: /v/ вү  /b/. PROCLITIC GUARD: the common function words o/os/no/nos/do/dos/ao/aos/pelo/pelos/um/uns are given word_exceptions with their correct [u]-vowel forms (oвҶ’[u], noвҶ’[nu]); the word-level stress detector mis-marks these monosyllabic clitics as stressed, so without the override ALG_U_FRONTING would wrongly front the article/contraction vowel to [y]. Genuine stressed lexical /u/ still fronts (tudoвҶ’[ЛҲtydu], sulвҶ’[ЛҲsyЙ«]).

ancestors.notes: Standard European Portuguese; Algarvio diverges in sibilant voicing (sвҶ’К’ coda/final), ei/ou monophthongs, and open vowel qualities

sources.author: Cintra, L. F. Lindley
sources.title: Nova proposta de classificaГ§ГЈo dos dialectos galego-portugueses
sources.publisher: Boletim de Filologia (Centro de Estudos FilolГіgicos, Lisboa), 22: 81вҖ“116
sources.notes: Foundational classification of European Portuguese dialects. Delimits the Barlavento Algarvio by the isogloss of stressed-/u/ palatalisation (вҖҳa palatalizaГ§ГЈo, em maior ou menor grau, da vogal tГіnica uвҖҷ) and lists final unstressed high-vowel deletion (вҖҳa queda das vogais finais -u, -i (ou -e), outro dos fenГіmenos mais tГӯpicos desta regiГЈoвҖҷ) as a central-southern (Alto Alentejo / Beira Baixa) feature.

sources.author: Brissos, Fernando
sources.title: New insights into Portuguese central-southern dialects: understanding their present and past forms through acoustic data from stressed vowels
sources.notes: Acoustic study of central-southern EP vowel systems. Documents Algarvio stressed /u/ вҶ’ [y] ([tЛҲyd] вҖҳtudoвҖҷ, [lЛҲymЙЁ] вҖҳlumeвҖҷ) and final unstressed [u] deletion/reduction ([sЛҲЕ“Кғt] вҖҳcestoвҖҷ); cites LГјdtke (1957) and Maia (1975вҖ“78) that the /u/ palatalisation is stressed-only.

sources.author: Segura da Cruz, Maria LuГӯsa
sources.title: La palatalisation de [u] dans le В«Barlavento AlgarvioВ»
sources.publisher: Espaces romans (Г©tudes de dialectologie et de gГ©olinguistique offertes Г  Gaston Tuaillon), II: 434вҖ“456. Grenoble: UniversitГ© Stendhal / ELLUG
sources.notes: Monograph devoted to the stressed-/u/ вҶ’ [y] fronting isogloss of the western (Barlavento) Algarve.

sources.author: Portuguese With Leo
sources.title: The 8 Portuguese accents of Portugal
sources.notes: Native-speaker EP accent breakdown (NOT academic). Identifies the Algarve as having the strongest /К’/-for-/z/ external sandhi (word-final s + vowel-initial word вҶ’ [К’]): 'Tajaver' (estГЎs a ver), 'quijentrar', 'majaГЎgua', 'muitojamigos', 'Г©jo nГәmero um'; shared (weaker) by SГЈo Miguel (Azores), variable in Coimbra, absent in Lisbon and the North (which keep [z]). Grounds the external-sandhi reading of the word-final [К’] this spec already models via its positional word_final map. A page-pinned academic source for the prevocalic-[К’] sandhi specifically was not located; the [К’]-for-final-/s/ voicing sits within the documented southern sibilant behaviour.

sources.author: BolГ©o, M.P.
sources.title: Estudos de linguГӯstica portuguesa e romГўnica

stress.notes: Acordo OrtogrГЎfico 1990 conventions: paroxytone default; written accents override; final -i/-u/-r/-l/-z/-x/-im/-om/-um/-ns and nasal diphthongs attract final stress. Unmarked -em/-am stay paroxytone (homem, falam).

allophone_rules.notes: Barlavento Algarvio fronting of the stressed labio-velar /u/ to the front rounded [y] вҖ” the defining and most salient feature of the western Algarve. Cintra (1971) delimits the sub-dialect by exactly this isogloss (вҖҳa palatalizaГ§ГЈo, em maior ou menor grau, da vogal tГіnica uвҖҷ); Brissos (2014) gives [tЛҲyd] вҖҳtudoвҖҷ and [lЛҲymЙЁ] вҖҳlumeвҖҷ; Segura da Cruz (1989) is a study dedicated to it. Restricted to the STRESSED nucleus (stress=вҖҳstressedвҖҷ): it does not apply to unstressed /u/ (LГјdtke 1957; Maia 1975вҖ“78, via Cintra 1971 and Brissos 2014), so the condition also blocks it from feeding on the inherited pt-PT unstressed /o/вҶ’[u] reduction. NOTES-ONLY (not modelled): the wider central-southern chain shift вҖ” stressed [a]вҶ’[Йӣ] in high-vowel/palatal contexts and [Й”]вҶ’[oМқ] raising (Brissos 2014) вҖ” is context-conditioned and left to notes to avoid a cascade collapse of the vowel space; only the categorical /u/вҶ’[y] is realised here.

location.notes: the Algarve вҖ” Portugal's southernmost region (Faro, PortimГЈo, Lagos). [Wikidata Q26831 P625]
